revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土用]][[ko:토용]]
{{Cite Source|date=July 2019|qq=}} '''Doyō''' (土用) is a {{ill|Calendar|en|Calendar|qid=Q12132|lt=calendar|1=Calendar}} {{ill|seasonal marker|ja|雑節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasonal marker|1=Zassetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q64015453}} derived from the {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|en|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|qid=Q218652|lt=Five Elements (Wuxing)|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}}. It refers to the period of approximately 18 days preceding each of the {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|qid=Q1190993|lt=four seasonal divisions|1=Solar term}}—{{ill|Liqiu|en|Liqiu|qid=Q1147421|lt=Risshū (Beginning of Autumn)|1=Liqiu}}, {{ill|Lidong|en|Lidong|qid=Q1191018|lt=Rittō (Beginning of Winter)|1=Lidong}}, {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|qid=Q1364685|lt=Risshun|1=Lichun}}, and {{ill|Lixia|en|Lixia|qid=Q1191007|lt=Lixia|1=Lixia}}.<ref name="koyomi_wiki">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/B5A8C0E12FBBA8C0E1A4C8A4CFA1A9.html|title=What is Zassetsu? - Seasonal Markers|website=Calendar Wiki|publisher={{ill|National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|en|National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|qid=Q1327713|lt=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|1=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}} Calendar Calculation Office|accessdate=August 31, 2024|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|ja|url=https://biz-journal.jp/company/post_24324.html|title=How did 3,000 yen Unagi Bento sell on Doyō no Ushi Day? Is Chinese eel enough for quality? |publisher=Business Journal|date=August 8, 2018|accessdate=November 23, 2020|qq=}}</ref> While Doyō occurs four times a year, it most commonly refers to the {{ill|summer Doyō|ja|夏の土用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer Doyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 土用|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 土用|1=Doyō (season)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11423778}}, right before the start of autumn<ref>{{Cite book|ja|editor=Masayoshi Nishikado|title=Encyclopedia of Annual Events|page=545|date=May 23, 1958|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|qq=}}</ref>. A traditional custom during this period is to eat {{ill|Unagi|en|Unagi|qid=Q1422437|lt=unagi (eel)|1=Unagi}} on {{ill|Midsummer Ox Day|en|Midsummer Ox Day|qid=Q16642915|lt=Doyō no Ushi Day|1=Midsummer Ox Day}} to regain stamina in the summer heat<ref name="saijiki">{{Cite book|ja|author1=Yuko Shikama|author2=Miho Nagao|author3=Satsuki Uchiyama|title=Seasonal Almanac: A Sensory Guide to Japanese Traditions|series=Contemporary Terminology Supplement 2008 Edition|date=January 1, 2008|publisher=Jiyu Kokuminsha|page=62|qq=}}</ref>. The first day of Doyō is called '''Doyō no Iri''' (土用の入り), and the last day is called '''Doyō no Ake''' (土用の明け)<ref name="koyomi_wiki"/>. The end of Doyō often coincides with {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|qid=Q746798|lt=Setsubun|1=Setsubun}}, which marks the day before the start of the new season, though it commonly refers to the day before the beginning of spring today<ref name="koyomi_wiki"/>. == History == In the {{ill|Five Elements theory|ja|五行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Elements theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五行思想|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五行思想|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q218652}}, the seasons are associated with different elements: wood for spring, fire for summer, metal for autumn, and water for winter<ref name="koyomi_wiki"/>. The remaining element, earth, is assigned to transitional periods between the seasons, referred to as '''Doyō''' (literally "earth-use") derived from ''Doō Yōji'' (土旺用事)<ref name="koyomi_wiki"/><ref name="kaiho">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www1.kaiho.mlit.go.jp/KOHO/faq/reki/rekicyuu.html|title=Various Calendar Notes - Astronomical and Calendar Information|website={{ill|Japan Coast Guard|en|Japan Coast Guard|qid=Q565418|lt=Japan Coast Guard|1=Japan Coast Guard}} Hydrographic and Oceanographic Department|accessdate=August 31, 2024|qq=}}</ref>. During Doyō, it was traditionally considered inauspicious to disturb the earth through activities like digging or construction<ref name="kaiho"/>. However, projects already underway before Doyō could continue. There were also "non-restrictive days" called {{ill|Doyō no Mabi|ja|土用の間日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doyō no Mabi|1=Doyō no Mabi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11423779}} when such taboos did not apply. == Customs == Doyō is associated with various seasonal foods believed to boost health: * '''{{ill|Doyō Mochi|ja|土用餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doyō Mochi|1=Doyō mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109596821}}''': A type of rice cake eaten for energy. * '''Doyō Shijimi''': Freshwater clams harvested during Doyō, considered nutritious. * '''Doyō Tamago''': Eggs laid by chickens during the Doyō period<ref name="dia">[https://dia.or.jp/disperse/dianews/pdf/dianews_no90_07.pdf Japanese Food Almanac] Dia Foundation for Aging Research, accessed July 28, 2021.</ref><ref>[http://www.ibarahp.jp/medical/pdf/eiyou_dayori_03.pdf Doyō no Ushi Day] Ibara City Hospital, accessed July 28, 2021.</ref>. Each Doyō period has specific foods associated with good fortune: * Spring: Foods starting with "i" or white-colored foods. * Summer: Foods starting with "u" (like unagi) or black-colored foods. * Autumn: Foods starting with "t" or blue-colored foods. * Winter: Foods starting with "h" or red-colored foods<ref name="saijiki"/><ref name="dia"/>.
== See Also == * {{ill|Midsummer Ox Day|en|Midsummer Ox Day|qid=Q16642915|lt=Doyō no Ushi Day|1=Midsummer Ox Day}} * {{ill|Doyō no Mabi|ja|土用の間日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doyō no Mabi|1=Doyō no Mabi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11423779}} * {{ill|Doyō Waves|ja|土用波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doyō Waves|1=Doyō nami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11423782}} * {{ill|Winter Doyō no Ushi Day|ja|寒の土用の丑の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Doyō no Ushi Day|1=Midwinter Day of the Ox|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11457357}}
{{translated page|ja|土用|version=103224178|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:土用]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ko:토용]] {{wikidata link|Q11423778}} [[Category:Seasonal terms for summer]] [[Category:Seasons]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zassetsu]]
